--- 
title: "Intervention Mapping Workbook"
author: "Nicole, GJ & Rob"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
output: bookdown::gitbook
documentclass: book
bibliography: [bibliography.bib]
biblio-style: apalike
link-citations: yes
description: "The general aim of the workshop is to practice the steps of the intervention mapping protocol in groups. The structure of the workshop is guided by the IM protocol, as described in the book PLANNING HEALTH PROMOTION PROGRAMS by Bartholomew et al (2016). Below, a detailed outline is presented of working with the Intervention Mapping protocol. For each step of the protocol specific tasks are formulated."
---

# Introduction {-}

This book lives at https://intervention-mapping.gitlab.io/uva-im

## Aim of the workshop

The general aim of the workshop is to practice the steps of the intervention mapping protocol in groups. The structure of the workshop is guided by the IM protocol, as described in the book PLANNING HEALTH PROMOTION PROGRAMS by Bartholomew et al (2016). Below, a detailed outline is presented of working with the Intervention Mapping protocol. For each step of the protocol specific tasks are formulated. 

### Oral presentations

You are performing the tasks in little groups. While working on the tasks, you make notes, and Powerpoint slides of the progress that is made by your group. You present these in the plenary sessions to receive feedback from the IM experts.

By Friday, your group will have gone through all the steps, and you will present a pptx presentation of the IM steps you have taken, ending up in your intervention, the possible ways the intervention should be designed and implemented and you provide the necessary theoretical background. You will receive expert feedback on the results of your intervention mapping and will have time to process that feedback to improve the plans. The final pptx slides must be handed in at the end of the workshop week (deadline Friday 6PM). 

### Grade

The Friday presentation will be evaluated as a group product. Team members will thus get the same grades. The presentation and final pptx slides count for 20% of your final course mark. The presentation will be evaluated by prof dr Rob Ruiter, dr GJ Peters and dr Nicole Oei. 
Submit the presentation as pdf file and send it to the following email address: N.Y.L.Oei@uva.nl.

## Your Guest Lecturers

### Rob Ruiter

![Rob Ruiter](img/Rob.jpg)

Prof Dr Rob Ruiter (MSc Health Sciences, 1995; PhD Psychology, 2000 Maastricht University) is an expert in Intervention Mapping, and coauthor of the book we use. He is a professor of applied psychology and head of department of Work and Social Psychology at the Faculty of Psychology and Neurocience, Maastricht University. The research projects he is involved in include studies into changing of risky health and safety behavior and developing and testing behavior change programs in the areas of blood donation, maternal health, safe sex promotion, and traffic safety using Intervention Mapping. Since 1995, Rob is involved in PhD projects in Southern and Eastern Africa, mostly related to prevention of infectious diseases such as HIV/AIDS and malaria. These projects have the goal to develop theory- and evidence-based health education materials while training young students to become fully qualified public health researchers. See his publications [here](https://scholar.google.nl/citations?user=F2R3dN0AAAAJ&hl=en&oi=sra).

We are very proud of the fact that Rob Ruiter was actively involved in this workshop from the start of the Development and Health mastertrack in 2017 With his indispensable expert knowledge in the field of Intervention Mapping.
  
### Gjalt-Jorn Peters
 
![GJ](img/GJ.jpg)

For the third year in a row, we are happy to welcome Dr Gjalt-Jorn Peters (PhD Psychology, 2008, Maastricht University). GJ is a behavior change professional on the cross-section between behavior change science (psychology), methodology and statistics and technology (ICT) and an assistant professor at the Dutch Open University. His PhD thesis was on the determinants of ecstasy use and related behaviors. You can read it [here](http://phdthesis.nl/gjalt-jorn). His teaching during his PhD and post-doc projects at Maastricht University included health behavior change, specifically Intervention Mapping.

He owns a behavior change research and intervention development company called [“Greater Good”](https://greatergood.eu/). With Greater Good, he develops online tools, for instance to help people to develop theory- and evidence-based behavior change interventions. He mainly investigates methods for behavior change, determinants of recreational substance use, smoking, and 'partying safely'. Do take a look at [his page on effective behavior change](https://effectivebehaviorchange.com).
